meals.name: Sheet Pan Lemon Chicken & Broccoli
meals.description: One-pan dinner with bright lemony chicken.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F and line a large sheet pan with parchment.
Cut broccoli into florets and chicken breasts into 1-inch pieces. Toss both with 3 tbsp olive oil, minced garlic, salt, and pepper.
Spread chicken and broccoli in a single layer on the sheet pan. Squeeze one lemon over the top.
Roast 20–25 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F and broccoli edges are crispy.
Finish with fresh lemon wedges and serve.

meals.name: Ground Beef Tacos
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high. Brown 1 lb ground beef, breaking it up, about 7 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add taco seasoning and 1/3 cup water. Simmer 3–4 minutes until thickened.
Meanwhile, warm taco shells in a 350°F oven for 5 minutes. Shred lettuce and dice tomatoes.
Assemble tacos with beef, cheese, lettuce, and tomatoes. Serve with salsa.

meals.name: Spaghetti with Red Sauce & Garlic Bread
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Cook spaghetti according to package, usually 9 minutes.
Warm marinara sauce in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Slice Italian bread lengthwise. Mix softened butter with minced garlic and spread on cut sides.
Broil bread on a sheet pan 2–3 minutes until golden.
Drain pasta, top with sauce, garnish with parmesan, and serve with garlic bread.

meals.name: Leftover night — no cooking
meals.description: Clear out Monday through Wednesday's leftovers.

meals.name: Sheet Pan Salmon with Roasted Potatoes
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F. Halve baby potatoes and toss with olive oil, rosemary, salt, and pepper.
Spread potatoes on a sheet pan and roast for 15 minutes.
Push potatoes to one side, add salmon fillets skin-side down, brush with olive oil, squeeze lemon over top.
Roast 12–15 more minutes until salmon flakes easily with a fork (internal temp 145°F).
Serve with extra lemon wedges.

meals.name: Homemade Pizza Night
meals.description: Let the kids build their own pies.
instructions: Preheat oven to 475°F. If using a pizza stone, heat it in the oven.
Stretch each dough ball on a floured surface into a 12-inch round.
Transfer to a parchment-lined pizza peel or sheet pan. Spread sauce, sprinkle mozzarella, add pepperoni and any toppings.
Bake 10–12 minutes until crust is golden and cheese is bubbly.
Rest 2 minutes, slice, and serve.

meals.name: Slow-Cooker Chili
meals.description: Hands-off Sunday meal, ready in the afternoon.
instructions: In a skillet over medium-high, brown 1 lb ground beef with diced onion, about 8 minutes. Drain.
Transfer beef and onion to a slow cooker. Add drained beans, diced tomatoes with liquid, chili powder, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Cook on LOW for 6 hours or HIGH for 3–4 hours.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve with sour cream, shredded cheese, and cornbread if desired.
